original_abstract: This recommendation provides a formal definition for the term “halogen bond,” identifying it as a specific category of noncovalent interaction. It characterizes the halogen bond as a net attractive engagement between an electrophilic region on a halogen atom and a nucleophilic region within the same or a separate molecular entity. Consistent with IUPAC nomenclature, this definition distinguishes these interactions from those where a halogen acts as a nucleophile, such as in hydrogen bonding. In addition to the formal definition, this document outlines the structural, energetic, and spectroscopic features that serve as evidence for the presence of a halogen bond.
humanized_abstract: Recommendation Review Article Definition of the halogen bond (IUPAC recommendation) DOI: 10.1039/C5SC00013F A definition of the term halogen bond is provided. The halogen bond is one type of noncovalent interaction defined as a net attractive force between an electrophilic site on a halogen atom and a nucleophilic site on another atom, group or molecule. In accordance with current IUPAC nomenclature a description of a halogen atom participating in a hydrogen bond is distinguished from a description of a halogen bond. In addition to the definition of the term halogen bond the structural, energetic and spectroscopic evidence for the occurrence of a halogen bond is summarized.
